message:
docs(e2e): fix misleading bead reference in system.disk bypass-var comment

Third fresh-eyes pass caught a wrong cross-reference: the comment in
scenario_system_disk_default claimed "severity-bump is tracked
separately in 8kh4", but bead 8kh4 is the mkswap-coverage gap, NOT
the severity-promotion question. The severity-bump (promote
mkfs/dd-device/etc to Critical) is a separate scope question that
isn't tracked by any bead today.

Reworded the comment to:
  - Keep the explanation of why DCG_BYPASS=1 currently relaxes
    these rules (they're High severity, matching the contract used
    by the rest of the core packs).
  - Note that nqhi.8's "are critical-tier" parenthetical was the
    bead author's aspiration, not a description of current behavior.
  - State plainly that severity promotion is a separate scope
    question with no bead yet, instead of pointing at the wrong one.

No code change. All checks still green: 2720 lib tests pass, 485 e2e
assertions pass, cargo fmt + clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -788,10 +788,12 @@ scenario_system_disk_default() {
     assert_allowed 'fdisk -l'
 
     # Bypass-var contract: falsy values must NOT bypass; truthy values
-    # MUST bypass. (system.disk rules are currently High severity, so
-    # DCG_BYPASS=1 does relax them — same contract as the rest of the
-    # core packs. The "Critical-tier" parenthetical in the bead title
-    # was aspirational; severity-bump is tracked separately in 8kh4.)
+    # MUST bypass. system.disk rules are currently High severity in the
+    # pack, so DCG_BYPASS=1 does relax them — this matches the contract
+    # used by the rest of the core packs. (The "are critical-tier"
+    # parenthetical in nqhi.8's title was aspirational; promoting these
+    # to Critical severity is a separate scope question outside
+    # default-enablement and is not tracked by any bead yet.)
     assert_blocked_under_falsy_bypass  'mkfs.ext4 /dev/sda1'              'system.disk:mkfs'
     assert_blocked_under_falsy_bypass  'dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/sda bs=1M' 'system.disk:dd-device'
     assert_allowed_under_truthy_bypass 'mkfs.ext4 /dev/sda1'
